objectives: Evaluate test coverage and quality across all 22 crates in the mx20022 payments runtime workspace
constraints: Assess all #[cfg(test)] sections, tests/ dir, harness/, fixtures/, benches/, and CI config
important_facts: tests/harness/ is empty — no integration test harness exists
tests/fixtures/ is empty — no shared ISO 20022 XML fixtures exist
benches/ directory is empty — no benchmarks exist
CI workflow (ci.yml) runs only: cargo fmt --check and cargo test --workspace
No proptest, quickcheck, or criterion in any Cargo.toml
tests_missing.rs files in runtime-core and store-sqlite are NOT wired into the build (marked with explicit comment)
mx20022-store-postgres and mx20022-store-rocksdb have zero tests
mx20022-runtime-core context.rs and state.rs have no inline tests (covered partially by tests_missing.rs which IS wired via lib.rs)
22 workspace members total; ~14 have at least one test
Single combined test in correlation covers register+match+timeout in one function — not separated
Session manager missing tests for reconnect_after_failure, sequence rollback, connect-from-wrong-state
CryptoService has only one encrypt/decrypt roundtrip test — missing empty key, wrong key decryption, unsupported algorithm
No mock Store implementation — tests use real SqliteStore (in-memory) as the integration vehicle
decisions_made: Categorized each crate as: tested / partially tested / untested
risks_issues: tests_missing.rs for store-sqlite is explicitly NOT wired in (comment says so) — 5 high-value tests are dead code
Postgres and RocksDB store backends have zero test coverage — production backends are unvalidated
No benchmark coverage on critical path (TransactionManager.process, schema validation)
No property-based testing on ISO 20022 field validators (BIC, IBAN, UETR format checks)
CI only runs cargo test — no coverage reporting, no clippy, no deny

iteration.summary: Performed a full read of all 55 Rust source files across 22 crates, the CI workflow, migrations, seeds, and directory structure. Found a workspace with solid unit test coverage in the business logic layer (participants, correlation, session, crypto, config, admin) but with critical gaps in infrastructure: the Postgres and RocksDB store backends are completely untested, all message-transport channels (HTTP, Kafka, AMQP, NATS) have no tests, the tests/ harness and fixtures directories are empty stubs, the benches/ directory is empty, and there is no property-based testing anywhere. The tests_missing.rs file for the SQLite store contains 5 well-designed tests but is explicitly excluded from the build. CI is minimal — format check plus cargo test, no coverage gate, no clippy, no cargo-deny.
outputs_produced: Structured test coverage report (returned to user)
next_actions: Wire tests_missing.rs into the build for store-sqlite
Add Postgres integration tests using testcontainers-rs
Add proptest coverage for BIC/IBAN/UETR/currency validation rules
Add benchmarks for TransactionManager.process with criterion
Add at least one realistic ISO 20022 XML fixture to tests/fixtures/
